angle of depression.s: The angle you look down from a higher spot.
angle of depression.r: Looking down from a balcony at someone below uses an angle of depression.

addition.s: Putting numbers together to make a bigger number.
addition.r: 3 plus 2 means 3 dots and 2 dots together = 5 dots. If you have 3 apples and someone gives you 2 more, you now have 5.

cone.s: A 3D shape with a round base and a pointy top.
cone.r: An ice cream cone is a cone.

speed.s: How fast something is moving, measured as distance travelled per unit of time.
speed.r: A car going 60 km per hour has a speed of 60 km/h.

column.s: A vertical line of entries in a matrix.
column.r: In a grid of numbers, a column goes up and down.

pyramid.s: A 3D shape with a polygon base and pointy top.
pyramid.r: The pyramids of Egypt are shaped like this.

share.s: A small piece of a company that you can buy.
share.m: Share = a piece of a company.
share.r: If a company has 100 shares, each share means 1% ownership.

minimum.s: The smallest value of a function in a given range.
minimum.r: The bottom of a valley is the minimum.

sum.s: The answer you get when you add numbers.

perpendicular bisector.s: A line that cuts another line in half at 90 degrees.
perpendicular bisector.r: The line down the middle of a square is a perpendicular bisector.

hyperbola.s: Two U-shapes facing away from each other.
hyperbola.r: The shadow of a lampshade at an angle can make a hyperbolic curve.

antiderivative.s: A function whose derivative is the original function.
antiderivative.r: x squared is the antiderivative of 2x.

percentage.s: A number written out of 100, shown with the % sign.
percentage.r: If you scored 50 out of 100 on a test, that is 50 percent.

log.s: Short for logarithm - the power a base must be raised to give a number.
log.r: Log base 10 of 100 is 2 because 10 squared = 100.

function.s: A rule that takes one input and gives one output.
function.r: A vending machine is a function - same input gives same snack.

BODMAS.s: The order to follow in a calculation: Brackets, Orders, Divide, Multiply, Add, Subtract.
BODMAS.r: For 2 + 3 times 4, BODMAS says multiply first: 2 + 12 = 14.

sine.s: A ratio of sides in a right-angled triangle: opposite over hypotenuse.
sine.r: In a triangle, sin of the angle = opposite divided by hypotenuse.

term.s: A single part of an expression, separated by plus or minus.
term.r: In 3x + 5, the 3x and the 5 are two separate terms.

angle of elevation.s: The angle you look up from horizontal to see something higher.
angle of elevation.r: Looking up at a kite in the sky uses an angle of elevation.

triangle.s: A flat shape with 3 straight sides.
triangle.r: A slice of sandwich is a triangle.

complex number.s: A number with a real part and an imaginary part.
complex number.r: Engineers use complex numbers to design electrical circuits.

interior angle.s: An angle found inside a shape.
interior angle.r: The corners inside a triangle are its interior angles.

element.s: One of the items in a set.
element.r: In the set {1,2,3}, each number is an element.

gradient.s: How steep a line is, measured as rise over run.
gradient.r: A steep hill has a bigger gradient than a gentle slope.

circle.r: A clock face is a circle.

probability.s: How likely something is to happen.
probability.r: When flipping a coin, the probability of heads is 1 out of 2.

independent.s: When one event does not change the chance of another.
independent.r: Flipping a coin twice - the events are independent.

integer.s: A whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero.

biased.s: When a test favours one result more than others.
biased.r: A weighted coin is biased toward one side.

standard form.s: A way to write very big or small numbers cleanly.
standard form.r: 3000 written in standard form is 3 times 10 cubed.

surface area.s: The total area of all the faces of a 3D shape added together.
surface area.r: Wrapping paper for a box - you need surface area worth of paper.

universal set.s: The set of all items under consideration.
universal set.r: When studying classroom pets, the universal set is every possible pet.

cube.s: A 3D shape with six identical square faces.

formula.s: A rule written with variables to find an answer.
formula.r: A equals pi times r squared is the formula for the area of a circle.

displacement.s: The change in position from start to end.
displacement.r: If you walk 3 km north, your displacement is 3 km north.

linear.s: Describes a relationship whose graph is a straight line.

pie chart.s: A circle cut into slices to show how a total is split.
pie chart.r: A pie chart of pocket money by category is round.

circumference.s: The distance all the way around a circle.
circumference.r: A race around a circular track - one lap is the circumference.

parallel.s: Lines that stay the same distance apart forever.
parallel.r: Railway tracks are parallel - they never meet.

cubic.s: An expression where the highest power of x is 3.

subset.s: A set whose every element also belongs to another set.
subset.r: The set of even numbers is a subset of all integers.

compass rose.s: A star-shaped diagram of the directions on a map.
compass rose.m: Compass rose = the directions star on a map.
compass rose.r: The compass rose on a map shows North, South, East, West and the four diagonal directions.

octagon.s: A flat shape with 8 straight sides.
octagon.r: A stop sign is an octagon.

trial.s: One run of a probability experiment.
trial.r: Each coin flip is one trial.

prime.s: A whole number bigger than 1 with only two factors: 1 and itself.
prime.r: 5 is prime - only 1 times 5 makes it.

solve.s: To find the value of a variable that makes an equation true.

set.s: A collection of distinct items, considered as one object.
set.r: The set of red things could be {apple, fire truck, heart}.

discriminant.s: A number from a quadratic that tells how many solutions it has.
discriminant.r: If positive, the quadratic has two real solutions.

inverse sine.s: The angle whose sine is a given number, also called arcsin.
inverse sine.r: If sin(x) = 0.5, the inverse sine gives x = 30 degrees.

square root.s: The number that, multiplied by itself, gives the original.
square root.r: The square root of 9 is 3 because 3 times 3 = 9.

like terms.s: Terms that have the same variables to the same powers.
like terms.r: 3x and 5x are like terms - both have just x.

scalar.s: A quantity with only size, no direction.

factorise.s: To write an expression as a product of its factors.

inverse cosine.s: The angle whose cosine is a given number, also called arccos.
inverse cosine.r: If cos(x) = 0.5, the inverse cosine gives x = 60 degrees.

turning point.s: A point on a curve where it changes direction.
turning point.r: The top of a hill in a roller-coaster graph is a turning point.

factor.s: A whole number that divides another number with nothing left over.
factor.r: 4 is a factor of 12 because 12 split into 4 groups is exactly 3.

exterior angle.s: An angle outside a shape, made by extending one side.
exterior angle.r: When you extend a fence line, the bend outside is an exterior angle.

order.s: The size of a matrix, written as rows by columns.
order.r: A 3 by 2 matrix has 3 rows and 2 columns.

divisor.r: In 12 divided by 3, the 3 is the divisor.

revenue.s: The total amount of money received from sales before any costs are taken out.
revenue.r: A coffee shop that takes 500 pounds in a day has 500 pounds of revenue.

scientific notation.r: 6,020,000 in scientific notation is 6.02 times 10 to the 6.

similar.s: Same shape but possibly different sizes.
similar.r: A small photo and the same photo blown up big are similar.

hypotenuse.s: The longest side of a right-angled triangle.
hypotenuse.r: On a ladder leaning against a wall, the ladder itself is the hypotenuse.

ratio.s: A way to compare two quantities.
ratio.r: If there are 3 boys and 2 girls, the ratio is 3 to 2.

prism.s: A 3D shape with two matching polygon ends and flat sides.
prism.r: A bar of chocolate is like a prism.

approximation.s: A number close to the exact value but easier to use.
approximation.r: Pi is about 3.14 is an approximation.

reciprocal.s: The number you multiply by to get 1.
reciprocal.r: The reciprocal of 4 is 1/4 because 4 times 1/4 = 1.

definite integral.s: An integral with start and end values, giving exact area.
definite integral.r: The integral from 0 to 2 of x dx = 2 - a definite area.

SSS.s: Three matching sides = the triangles are exactly the same.
SSS.r: If you measure three sides of one triangle and three sides of another and they all match, the two triangles are identical copies.

multiplication.s: Adding a number to itself many times.

consecutive.s: Numbers that follow each other with no gaps.

trapezium.s: A four-sided shape with one pair of parallel sides.
trapezium.r: A table with slanted legs is shaped like a trapezium.

identity matrix.s: A square matrix with 1s on the diagonal and 0s elsewhere.
identity matrix.r: It is the matrix version of the number 1.

ASA.s: Two angles and the side between them = the triangles match.
ASA.r: If you know two angles and the side they share, you can reconstruct the triangle exactly.

rotational symmetry.s: When a shape looks the same after being rotated.
rotational symmetry.r: A starfish looks the same after turning a bit - that is rotational symmetry.

midpoint.s: The point exactly halfway along a line.
midpoint.r: The center mark on a ruler shows the midpoint of a line.

inverse tangent.s: The angle whose tangent is a given number, also called arctan.
inverse tangent.r: If tan(x) = 1, the inverse tangent gives x = 45 degrees.

scalene triangle.s: A triangle where all three sides are different lengths.
scalene triangle.r: A random triangle drawn freehand is usually scalene.

net.s: A flat layout that folds up into a 3D shape.
net.r: The unfolded cardboard of a cereal box is its net.

right-angled triangle.s: A triangle with one corner that is exactly 90 degrees.
right-angled triangle.r: The triangle made by a ladder, a wall, and the ground is right-angled.

output.s: The number that comes out of a function.
output.r: In f(x) = 2x, when you put in x = 3, the output is 6.

variable.s: A letter that stands in for an unknown number.
variable.r: In x + 5 = 10, the x is the variable we are trying to find.

right angle.r: The corner of a piece of paper is a right angle.

magnitude.s: The size or length of a vector.
magnitude.r: In 5 km east, the magnitude is 5 km.

expression.s: A sum of numbers and variables with no equals sign.
expression.r: 3x + 2 is an expression - it does not give an answer yet.

quotient.s: The answer you get when you divide one number by another.
quotient.r: The quotient of 20 divided by 4 is 5.

subtraction.s: Taking one number away from another.
subtraction.r: If you have 5 apples and give 2 away, you have 5 - 2 = 3 apples left.

cosine rule.s: A rule connecting sides and angles in any triangle.
cosine rule.m: Cosine rule = c squared = a squared + b squared - 2ab cos C.
cosine rule.r: Use the cosine rule when the sine rule cannot solve a triangle.

polygon.s: A closed flat shape made of straight sides.
polygon.r: Squares, triangles, and pentagons are all polygons.

quadratic.s: An expression where the highest power of x is 2.

equilateral triangle.s: A triangle with all three sides equal.
equilateral triangle.r: A road sign for warning is often an equilateral triangle.

recurring decimal.s: A decimal where the digits repeat forever.

whole number.s: A number with no fractions, including zero.

matrix.s: A grid of numbers arranged in rows and columns.
matrix.r: A spreadsheet with rows of grades is like a matrix.

significant figures.s: The meaningful digits in a number, starting from the first non-zero one.

segment.s: The region inside a circle between a chord and the arc.
segment.r: If you cut a circle with a straight line, you get two segments.

vertex.s: A corner point where lines or edges meet.
vertex.r: The pointy top of a triangle is a vertex.

scalar product.s: Another name for the dot product because the answer is a scalar.
scalar product.m: Scalar product = same as dot product.

reflex angle.s: An angle bigger than 180 but less than 360 degrees.
reflex angle.r: The outside of a star point can make a reflex angle.

trigonometry.s: The maths of the sides and angles of triangles.
trigonometry.r: Trig helps you find a tree height using its shadow.

interest.s: Money paid for borrowing money, or money earned for saving money.
interest.r: A bank pays you interest on the money you keep in your savings account.

gradient function.s: Another name for the derivative - it gives the slope of a curve.
gradient function.r: The gradient function of x squared is 2x.

sector.s: The region inside a circle between two radii.
sector.r: A pizza slice is a sector of the pizza.

negative.r: Negative 5 and negative 100 are both below zero.

centre.s: The middle point of a circle, the same distance from every edge.
centre.r: The dot in the middle of a dartboard is the centre.

hexagon.s: A flat shape with 6 straight sides.
hexagon.r: A snowflake is often a hexagon.

bearing.s: A direction measured as a clockwise angle from north.
bearing.r: A bearing of 090 means due east, like the sunrise.

prime number.s: A whole number bigger than 1 with only two factors: 1 and itself.
prime number.r: 7 is prime because only 1 times 7 makes it.

surd.s: A square root that cannot be turned into a neat whole number.
surd.r: Square root of 2 is a surd - about 1.414 but never exact.

simplify.s: To make an expression shorter by combining like parts.

cosine.s: A ratio in a right-angled triangle: adjacent over hypotenuse.
cosine.r: cos of an angle = side next to angle divided by hypotenuse.

y-intercept.s: The point where a line crosses the y-axis.
y-intercept.r: The starting height of a rocket graph is the y-intercept.

determinant.s: A single number computed from a square matrix.
determinant.r: It is like a fingerprint that tells you special things about the matrix.

histogram.s: A bar chart with no gaps, for grouped data.
histogram.r: A histogram of heights shows how many people are in each height range.

remainder.s: What is left over when a number does not divide evenly.

range.s: The biggest number minus the smallest number in a set.
range.r: If scores go from 40 to 90, the range is 50.

obtuse angle.s: An angle bigger than 90 but less than 180 degrees.
obtuse angle.r: The angle between clock hands at 10 and 2 is obtuse.

rational number.s: A number that can be written as a fraction of two integers.

integration.s: The reverse of differentiation, used to find areas.
integration.r: Integration can find the area under a curve.

mutually exclusive.s: Two events that cannot both happen.
mutually exclusive.r: Rolling a 1 and a 6 on one die are mutually exclusive.

frequency.r: The frequency of getting tails in 10 flips might be 6.

cylinder.s: A 3D shape with two flat circles and a curved side.
cylinder.r: A soup can is a cylinder.

scale factor.s: The number you multiply lengths by to make a shape bigger or smaller.
scale factor.r: If the scale factor is 2, every length doubles.

ellipse.s: A stretched-out circle - an oval shape.
ellipse.r: A racetrack or a flattened circle is shaped like an ellipse.

differentiation.s: Finding the rate of change of a function at every point.
differentiation.r: Differentiation can tell how fast a ball drops each second.

calculus.s: The maths of change and areas under curves.
calculus.r: Calculus can tell how fast a car is speeding up.

discount.s: An amount taken off the original price of something.
discount.r: A 20 percent discount on a 10 pound toy means you pay only 8 pounds.

rate.s: A comparison of two quantities measured in different units.
rate.r: Miles per hour is a rate - miles compared to hours.

time.s: A measure of how long something takes, in seconds, minutes, hours, or days.
time.r: A 100 metre race takes about 10 seconds - that is the time.

congruent.s: Exactly the same shape and same size.
congruent.r: Two identical cookie-cutter stars are congruent.

denominator.s: The bottom number in a fraction.
denominator.r: If a cake is split into 6 equal pieces, the 6 is the denominator.

mode.s: The number that appears most often in a set.
mode.r: In 2, 3, 3, 5, 7, the mode is 3 because 3 appears twice.

enlargement.s: Making a shape bigger or smaller using a scale factor.
enlargement.r: Photocopying a picture bigger is an enlargement.

west.s: The direction to your left when facing North.
west.r: The sun sets in the West. A bearing of 270 means due West.

principal.s: The original amount of money borrowed, invested, or saved, before any interest.
principal.r: If you invest 1000 pounds, the principal is 1000 pounds.

heptagon.s: A flat shape with 7 straight sides.
heptagon.r: A heptagon has 7 sides - it is rare in everyday life.

input.s: A number you put into a function.
input.r: In f(x) = 2x, the input is x and the output is the answer.

equivalent fraction.s: A different fraction with the same value.

exponential decay.s: A decrease that gets faster and faster over time.
exponential decay.r: A hot cup of tea cooling down follows exponential decay.

perimeter.s: The distance all the way around the outside.
perimeter.r: Imagine walking around your school playground fence - that total walk is the perimeter.

median.s: The middle value when numbers are put in order.
median.r: In 3, 5, 7 the median is 5 because it sits in the middle.

outcome.s: One possible result of an experiment.
outcome.r: Heads or tails are outcomes of flipping a coin.

union.s: A set that contains every element in either of two or more sets.

variation.s: When one number changes, another changes too in a pattern.
variation.m: Variation = one thing changing affects another.
variation.r: The more hours you work, the more money you earn - that is variation.

real number.s: Any number you can find on a number line.
real number.r: Every number from negative 100 to 3.14 is a real number.

rectangle.s: A four-sided shape with opposite sides equal and right-angle corners.
rectangle.r: A book cover is a rectangle.

sequence.s: A list of numbers that follows a rule.
sequence.r: 2, 4, 6, 8 is a sequence - each step adds 2.

depreciation.s: The decrease in the value of something over time.
depreciation.r: A new car loses value every year - that is depreciation.

stationary point.s: A point on a curve where the gradient is zero - briefly flat.
stationary point.r: The very top of a smooth hill is a stationary point.

composite.s: A whole number that has more than two factors.
composite.r: 6 is composite because 1, 2, 3, and 6 all divide it.

coefficient.s: The number in front of a variable.
coefficient.r: In 3x, the 3 is the coefficient - it means three copies of x.

row.s: A horizontal line of entries in a matrix.
row.r: In a grid of numbers, a row goes left to right.

irrational number.s: A number that cannot be written as a simple fraction.
irrational number.r: Pi is irrational - it never ends or repeats.

area.s: How much flat space a shape covers.
area.r: The rug on your bedroom floor - its size is the area.

RHS.s: Two right triangles with same hypotenuse + one side = the triangles match.
RHS.r: Used to prove two right-angled triangles are congruent when you know the hypotenuse and one leg of each.

acute angle.r: The tip of a sharp pencil makes an acute angle.

scatter graph.s: A graph of dots showing pairs of values.
scatter graph.r: A scatter graph of height vs weight shows how they relate.

complement.s: Everything in the universal set that is NOT in a given set.
complement.r: If the universal set is {1,2,3,4} and A is {1,2}, the complement of A is {3,4}.

radius.s: The distance from the center of a circle to its edge.
radius.r: Half the width of a pizza is its radius.

sample.s: A small group chosen from a bigger group.
sample.r: A poll of 100 voters is a sample of all voters.

loss.s: What happens when you spend more than you earn.
loss.r: If a shop pays 100 pounds for stock and sells it for only 80 pounds, that is a loss.

tessellation.s: A pattern of shapes fitted together with no gaps.
tessellation.r: Honeycomb in a beehive is a tessellation of hexagons.

cube root.s: The number that, times itself three times, gives the original.
cube root.r: The cube root of 27 is 3 because 3 times 3 times 3 = 27.

exponent.s: A small number showing how many times to multiply a base by itself.
exponent.r: In 2 to the power 3, the 3 says multiply 2 by itself three times.

chord.s: A straight line with both ends on a circle.
chord.r: A straight cut across a pizza is a chord.

indefinite integral.s: An integral with no limits, giving a family of answers.
indefinite integral.r: The integral of x dx = x squared over 2 plus C is an indefinite integral.

diagonal.s: A line that joins two corners that are not next to each other.
diagonal.r: The line from top-left to bottom-right of a square is a diagonal.

x-intercept.s: The point where a line crosses the x-axis.
x-intercept.r: The moment something reaches the ground in a graph is the x-intercept.

parabola.s: A U-shaped curve, like a smile or a bowl.
parabola.r: The path a ball makes when you throw it is a parabola - up then down.

focus.s: A special point inside a curve like an ellipse.
focus.m: Focus = special point inside a curve.
focus.r: In a satellite dish, the focus is where the signals collect to one spot.

terminating decimal.s: A decimal that stops after a few digits.

parallelogram.s: A four-sided shape where opposite sides run parallel.
parallelogram.r: A pushed-over square becomes a parallelogram.

binomial.s: A math expression with exactly two parts.
binomial.r: x + 5 is a binomial because it has exactly two parts.

limit.s: The value a function approaches as its input gets closer to something.
limit.r: As x gets near 0, 1 divided by x grows without limit.

inverse.r: Subtraction is the inverse of addition.

logarithm.s: The power a base must be raised to give a particular number.
logarithm.r: Log base 2 of 8 is 3 because 2 cubed = 8.

intersection.s: The set of elements that are in all of two or more sets.

polynomial.s: An expression made of variables and numbers combined with addition, subtraction, and multiplication.

directrix.s: A straight line that helps define a curve like a parabola.
directrix.m: Directrix = guide line for a curve.
directrix.r: The directrix is like a guide line that the curve never crosses.

compound interest.s: Interest calculated on the original amount plus any interest already earned.
compound interest.r: Compound interest makes savings grow faster because each year earns interest on top of last year.

south.s: The direction shown at the bottom of a map.
south.m: South = bottom of the map = 180 bearing.
south.r: A bearing of 180 means due South - the opposite of North.

sphere.s: A perfectly round 3D shape, like a ball.

fraction.s: A number written as one number over another.
fraction.r: 3/4 means 3 parts out of 4 equal parts.

vector.s: A quantity with both size and direction.
vector.r: An arrow showing 5 km east is a vector.

compass.s: A tool that shows you which way is North.
compass.r: A hiker uses a compass to find their way through a forest. The needle always points to magnetic North.

line of best fit.s: A straight line that passes closest to most points on a scatter graph.
line of best fit.r: Drawing a ruler through cloud of points gives a line of best fit.

edge.s: The line where two faces of a 3D shape meet.
edge.r: The 12 edges of a dice are its lines where faces meet.

round.s: To replace a number with a nearby simpler one.
round.r: Rounding 4.7 to the nearest whole gives 5.

improper fraction.s: A fraction where the top is bigger than the bottom.

identity.s: An equation that is true for every value of the variable.
identity.r: x + x = 2x is an identity - it always works.

expand.s: To multiply out the brackets in an expression.

numerator.s: The top number in a fraction.
numerator.r: If a pizza is cut into 8 slices and you take 3, the 3 is the numerator.

empty set.s: The set that contains no elements at all.
empty set.r: A box with nothing inside is like an empty set.

distance.s: How far apart two points are, or how far something has travelled.
distance.r: The distance from your home to school might be 2 km.

decimal.s: A number with a dot showing parts of a whole.

relative frequency.s: How often something happens compared to total trials.
relative frequency.r: If you flip tails 30 times in 100 flips, the relative frequency is 30/100.

equation.s: A math sentence with an equals sign.

dividend.s: Money a company pays you for owning its shares.
dividend.r: Apple pays shareholders a dividend every three months.

point of inflection.s: A point on a curve where it changes how it bends.
point of inflection.r: A roller coaster track may have a point of inflection between dips.

difference.s: The answer when you subtract one number from another.

kite.s: A four-sided shape with two pairs of equal sides next to each other.
kite.m: Kite = two pairs of equal sides.
kite.r: The toy kite flying in the sky has this shape.

rotation.s: Turning a shape around a fixed point.
rotation.r: Spinning a clock hand is a rotation.

average.s: A typical value for a set of numbers, usually the mean.

semicircle.r: A full moon in the sky looks like a semicircle.

integrate.s: To find the integral of a function.
integrate.r: You integrate 2x to get x squared.

translation.s: Sliding a shape without turning or flipping it.
translation.r: Moving a chess piece straight across is a translation.

product.s: The answer you get when you multiply numbers.

unit.s: A standard way of measuring something, like metres for length or seconds for time.
unit.r: Centimetres, kilograms, and degrees are all units.

exponential growth.s: Growth that gets faster and faster over time.
exponential growth.r: A bacteria colony under exponential growth can double every hour.

division.s: Sharing or splitting into equal groups.
division.r: 12 divided by 3 means split 12 cookies into 3 equal groups = 4 each.

mixed number.s: A whole number written next to a fraction.
mixed number.r: 2 and 3/4 is a mixed number - two and three-quarters.

volume.s: The amount of 3D space inside a solid.
volume.r: The volume of a fish tank tells you how much water fits inside.

inverse matrix.s: A matrix that undoes the effect of another matrix.
inverse matrix.r: Multiplying a matrix by its inverse gives the identity.

pentagon.s: A flat shape with 5 straight sides.
pentagon.r: The home plate in baseball looks like a pentagon.

proper fraction.s: A fraction where the top is smaller than the bottom.

line of symmetry.s: A line that splits a shape into two mirror halves.
line of symmetry.r: The line down the middle of a heart is a line of symmetry.

outlier.s: A value much bigger or smaller than the rest.
outlier.r: If most test scores are 60 to 80 and one is 5, that one is an outlier.

mean.s: The average of a set of numbers.
mean.r: If your last 4 test scores were 70, 80, 90, 100, the mean is 85.

absolute value.s: The size of a number ignoring whether it is positive or negative.
absolute value.r: The absolute value of negative 5 is 5 - we drop the sign.

bisector.s: A line that splits something into two equal parts.
bisector.r: A median of a triangle is a kind of bisector.

population.s: The whole group being studied in a survey.
population.r: If you survey 100 voters, the population is all the voters.

square.s: A four-sided shape with all sides equal and all corners square.
square.r: A chessboard square is a square.

root.s: A value of the variable that makes an equation equal zero.
root.r: For x squared - 4, the roots are 2 and -2.

inequality.s: A statement saying one value is bigger or smaller than another.
inequality.r: x > 5 means x is greater than 5.

tree diagram.s: A branching diagram showing all possible outcomes.
tree diagram.r: A tree diagram for two coin flips shows 4 outcomes.

Venn diagram.s: A diagram of overlapping circles that show relationships between sets.
Venn diagram.r: A Venn diagram can show who likes cats and who likes dogs.

angle.s: The amount of turn between two lines that meet at a point.
angle.r: The corner of an open book makes an angle.

transpose.s: A new matrix made by swapping rows and columns.
transpose.r: Transposing flips a matrix along its diagonal.

rhombus.s: A four-sided shape with all sides the same length.
rhombus.r: A diamond shape on a playing card is a rhombus.

derivative.s: A function that gives the rate of change of another function.
derivative.r: The derivative of x squared is 2x - it gives the slope.

adjacent.s: The side of a right-angled triangle next to a chosen angle (not the hypotenuse).
adjacent.r: The side next to a chosen corner is the adjacent side.

tangent.s: A straight line that just touches a curve at one point.
tangent.r: A wall just touching a round ball is a tangent line.

secant.s: A line that cuts through a circle at two points.
secant.r: A long stick passed straight through a hula hoop is a secant.

fair.s: When every outcome is equally likely.
fair.r: A normal coin is fair - heads and tails are equally likely.

reflection.s: Flipping a shape so it becomes a mirror image.
reflection.r: Your left hand and right hand are reflections of each other.

horizontal.s: Going side to side, parallel to the ground.
horizontal.r: The line of the horizon when you look out at sea is horizontal.

domain.s: All the numbers you are allowed to put into a function.
domain.r: In y = 1/x, the domain is all numbers except 0 - you cannot divide by zero.

integral.s: The result of integration, representing area under a curve.
integral.r: The integral of 2x is x squared.

event.s: Something that can happen, often in a probability experiment.
event.r: Rolling a 6 on a die is an event.

perpendicular.s: Two lines that cross at exactly a right angle.
perpendicular.r: The corner where two walls meet is perpendicular.

maximum.s: The largest value of a function in a given range.
maximum.r: The peak of a hill is its maximum height.

symmetry.s: When a shape can be folded so both halves match exactly.
symmetry.r: A butterfly has symmetry - both wings match.

simple interest.s: Interest calculated only on the original amount borrowed or invested.
simple interest.r: Simple interest on 100 pounds at 5 percent for 3 years is just 100 times 5 percent times 3.

digit.s: One of the single symbols used to write numbers.

arc.s: A curved piece of the edge of a circle.
arc.r: The slice of crust on a pizza is a circular arc.

profit.s: The money left over when you earn more than you spend.
profit.r: If you buy a toy for 5 pounds and sell it for 8 pounds, you made a profit of 3 pounds.

bar chart.s: A chart with rectangles of different heights to compare categories.
bar chart.r: A bar chart shows how many sales each shop made.

proportion.s: A statement that two ratios are equal.
proportion.r: If 2 oranges cost 50p, 4 oranges cost 100p - same proportion.

opposite.s: The side of a right-angled triangle across from a chosen angle.
opposite.r: The side far from a chosen corner is the opposite side.

sine rule.s: A rule connecting sides and angles in any triangle.
sine rule.r: Use the sine rule to find a missing side of any triangle.

asymptote.s: A line that a curve gets closer to but never touches.
asymptote.r: A car driving toward the horizon but never reaching it is like an asymptote.

quadrant.s: One of the four sections a graph is split into by its axes.
quadrant.r: The top-right section of a graph is Quadrant I.

differentiate.s: To find the derivative of a function.
differentiate.r: You differentiate x squared to get 2x.

east.s: The direction to your right when facing North.
east.r: The sun rises in the East. A bearing of 090 means due East.

north.s: The direction shown at the top of a map.
north.m: North = top of the map = 000 bearing.
north.r: On a map, North is at the top. A bearing of 000 means due North.

transformation.s: Any way of moving or resizing a shape.
transformation.r: Flipping, turning, or sliding a shape is a transformation.

tangent line.s: A ratio in a right-angled triangle: opposite over adjacent.
tangent line.r: tan of an angle = opposite divided by adjacent.

bearing angle.s: How far around the compass from North, measured clockwise.
bearing angle.r: A bearing of 090 means due East. A bearing of 225 is South-West (halfway between South and West).

line graph.s: A graph using lines to show how something changes over time.
line graph.r: A line graph of temperature through the day.

exponential.s: Describing growth or a function where a number is raised to a power with a variable.
exponential.r: Population growth can be exponential - doubling every few years.

SAS.s: Two sides and the angle between them = the triangles match.
SAS.r: If two sides and the angle between them are the same, the two triangles are identical - it is the most common way to prove congruence.

percent.s: A number written as parts per hundred, with a percent sign.

correlation.s: When two variables tend to change together.
correlation.r: Ice cream sales and temperature have a correlation.

stem and leaf.s: A way to sort data by splitting each number into a stem and leaf.
stem and leaf.r: 32 in a stem-and-leaf has stem 3 and leaf 2.

multiple.s: A number you get by multiplying a given number by a whole number.

quadrilateral.s: A flat shape with 4 straight sides.
quadrilateral.r: A window pane is usually a quadrilateral.

cross-section.s: The shape you see when you slice through a 3D object.
cross-section.r: Cutting a carrot gives a circle cross-section.

isosceles triangle.s: A triangle with two sides of the same length.
isosceles triangle.r: A slice of sandwich cut straight down is often isosceles.

diameter.s: The distance straight across a circle through the middle.
diameter.r: If a pizza is 30 cm across, the diameter is 30 cm.

face.s: A flat surface on the outside of a 3D shape.

dot product.s: A way to multiply two vectors, giving one number.
dot product.r: The dot product of (2,3) and (4,5) is 23.

solution.s: A value that satisfies an equation.
solution.r: In x + 1 = 4, the solution is x = 3.

natural number.s: A counting number: 1, 2, 3, and so on.
natural number.r: Counting fingers: 1, 2, 3 - these are natural numbers.

decimal place.s: The position of a digit to the right of a decimal point.
decimal place.m: Decimal place = position past the dot.
decimal place.r: In 3.14, the 1 is the first decimal place.

power.s: A small raised number showing repeated multiplication.
power.r: In 2 cubed, the 3 is the power.

base.s: The number being multiplied by itself in a power.
base.r: In 2 cubed, the 2 is the base.
